preview_text:
The model favors the Netherlands at 70% to win, with Tunisia assigned an 8% chance of victory. The underdog's upside is modest but not negligible; a draw is forecast at 22%, suggesting a competitive underlying match structure despite the clear favorite status.

The win-draw-loss spread reveals a contest tilted decisively toward the Netherlands, yet not a heavily lopsided affair. The draw probability near one-fifth implies meaningful competitive balance in stretches, even as Netherlands dominates the outright win column. The 61-point gap between the favorite's win odds and the underdog's reflects genuine asymmetry in expected quality or control.

The model and sportsbook consensus are closely aligned. Both place Netherlands between 63% and 70%, Tunisia between 8% and 15%, and the draw near 22%. Polymarket consensus tracks similarly. The model's Tunisia win estimate sits at the low end of the range—roughly 6 percentage points below the median book—a minor divergence that falls within typical forecasting noise.
